children.label: i
children.text: provides a predictable legal framework necessary for nuclear projects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensures prompt and equitable compensation in the event of a nuclear incident in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: the Price-Anderson Act, in effect, provides operators of nuclear powerplants with insurance for damage arising out of a nuclear incident and funds the insurance primarily through the assessment of a retrospective premium from each operator after the occurrence of a nuclear incident;

children.label: i
children.text: to provide a predictable legal framework necessary for nuclear energy projects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to ensure prompt and equitable compensation in the event of a nuclear incident;

children.label: D
children.text: the Convention benefits United States nuclear suppliers that face potentially unlimited liability for nuclear incidents that are not covered by the Price-Anderson Act by replacing a potentially open-ended liability with a predictable liability regime that, in effect, provides nuclear suppliers with insurance for damage arising out of such an incident;

children.label: E
children.text: the Convention also benefits United States nuclear facility operators that may be publicly liable for a Price-Anderson incident by providing an additional early source of funds to compensate damage arising out of the Price-Anderson incident;

children.label: F
children.text: the combined operation of the Convention, the Price-Anderson Act, and this section will augment the quantity of assured funds available for victims in a wider variety of nuclear incidents while reducing the potential liability of United States suppliers without increasing potential costs to United States operators;

children.label: G
children.text: the cost of those benefits is the obligation of the United States to contribute to the supplementary compensation fund established by the Convention;

children.label: i
children.text: upset settled expectations based on the liability regime established under the Price-Anderson Act; or

children.label: ii
children.text: shift to Federal taxpayers liability risks for nuclear incidents at foreign installations;

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to a Price-Anderson incident, funds already available under the Price-Anderson Act should be used; and

children.label: J
children.text: with respect to a nuclear incident outside the United States not covered by the Price-Anderson Act, a retrospective premium should be prorated among nuclear suppliers relieved from potential liability for which insurance is not available.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to a Price-Anderson incident, by using funds made available under to cover the contingent costs in a manner that neither increases the burdens nor decreases the benefits under ; andsection 2210 of this titlesection 2210 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to a covered incident outside the United States that is not a Price-Anderson incident, by allocating the contingent costs equitably, on the basis of risk, among the class of nuclear suppliers relieved by the Convention from the risk of potential liability resulting from any covered incident outside the United States.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 1 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.

children.heading: Contingent cost
children.label: 2 Contingent cost
children.text: The term “contingent cost” means the cost to the United States in the event of a covered incident the amount of which is equal to the amount of funds the United States is obligated to make available under paragraph 1(b) of Article III of the Convention.

children.heading: Convention
children.label: 3 Convention
children.text: The term “Convention” means the Convention on Supplementary Compensation for Nuclear Damage, done at Vienna on .September 12, 1997

children.heading: Covered incident
children.label: 4 Covered incident
children.text: The term “covered incident” means a nuclear incident the occurrence of which results in a request for funds pursuant to Article VII of the Convention.

children.heading: Covered installation
children.label: 5 Covered installation
children.text: The term “covered installation” means a nuclear installation at which the occurrence of a nuclear incident could result in a request for funds under Article VII of the Convention.

children.label: i
children.text: a United States person; and

children.label: I
children.text: is located in the United States; or

children.label: II
children.text: carries out an activity in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any agency or instrumentality of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: supplies facilities, equipment, fuel, services, or technology pertaining to the design, construction, operation, or decommissioning of a covered installation; or

children.label: B
children.text: transports nuclear materials that could result in a covered incident.

children.heading: Price-Anderson incident
children.label: 8 Price-Anderson incident
children.text: The term “Price-Anderson incident” means a covered incident for which would make funds available to compensate for public liability (as defined in ).section 2210 of this titlesection 2014 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 9 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “United States” has the meaning given the term in .section 2014 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico;

children.label: ii
children.text: any other territory or possession of the United States;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Canal Zone; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the waters of the United States territorial sea under Presidential Proclamation Number 5928, dated ( note).December 27, 198843 U.S.C. 1331

children.label: A
children.text: any individual who is a resident, national, or citizen of the United States (other than an individual residing outside of the United States and employed by a person who is not a United States person); and

children.label: B
children.text: any corporation, partnership, association, joint stock company, business trust, unincorporated organization, or sole proprietorship that is organized under the laws of the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Funds made available under shall be used to cover the contingent cost resulting from any Price-Anderson incident.section 2210 of this title

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 2 Effect
children.text: The use of funds pursuant to paragraph (1) shall not reduce the limitation on public liability established under .section 2210(e) of this title

children.heading: Effect on amount of public liability
children.label: d Effect on amount of public liability

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Funds made available to the United States under Article VII of the Convention with respect to a Price-Anderson incident shall be used to satisfy public liability resulting from the Price-Anderson incident.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of funds made available for the Price-Anderson incident under Article VII of the Convention; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of funds used under subsection (c) to cover the contingent cost resulting from the Price-Anderson incident.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), each nuclear supplier shall participate in a retrospective risk pooling program in accordance with this section to cover the contingent cost resulting from a covered incident outside the United States that is not a Price-Anderson incident.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The obligation of a nuclear supplier to participate in the retrospective risk pooling program shall be deferred until the United States is called on to provide funds pursuant to Article VII of the Convention with respect to a covered incident that is not a Price-Anderson incident.

children.heading: Amount of deferred payment
children.label: B Amount of deferred payment
children.text: The amount of a deferred payment of a nuclear supplier under subparagraph (A) shall be based on the risk-informed assessment formula determined under subparagraph (C).

children.label: I
children.text: the nature and intended purpose of the goods and services supplied by each nuclear supplier to each covered installation outside the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: the quantity of the goods and services supplied by each nuclear supplier to each covered installation outside the United States;

children.label: III
children.text: the hazards associated with the supplied goods and services if the goods and services fail to achieve the intended purposes;

children.label: IV
children.text: the hazards associated with the covered installation outside the United States to which the goods and services are supplied;

children.label: V
children.text: the legal, regulatory, and financial infrastructure associated with the covered installation outside the United States to which the goods and services are supplied; and

children.label: VI
children.text: the hazards associated with particular forms of transportation.

children.label: aa
children.text: goods and services with negligible risk;

children.label: bb
children.text: classes of goods and services not intended specifically for use in a nuclear installation;

children.label: cc
children.text: a nuclear supplier with a de minimis share of the contingent cost; and

children.label: dd
children.text: a nuclear supplier no longer in existence for which there is no identifiable successor; and

children.label: II
children.text: establish the period on which the risk assessment is based.

children.heading: Application
children.label: iii Application
children.text: In applying the formula, the Secretary shall not consider any covered installation or transportation for which funds would be available under .section 2210 of this title

children.heading: Report
children.label: iv Report
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , and every 5 years thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, a report on whether there is a need for continuation or amendment of this section, taking into account the effects of the implementation of the Convention on the United States nuclear industry and suppliers.December 19, 2007

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may collect information necessary for developing and implementing the formula for calculating the deferred payment of a nuclear supplier under subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: Provision of information
children.label: B Provision of information
children.text: Each nuclear supplier and other appropriate persons shall make available to the Secretary such information, reports, records, documents, and other data as the Secretary determines, by regulation, to be necessary or appropriate to develop and implement the formula under subsection (e)(2)(C).

children.heading: Private insurance
children.label: 2 Private insurance
children.text: The Secretary shall make available to nuclear suppliers, and insurers of nuclear suppliers, information to support the voluntary establishment and maintenance of private insurance against any risk for which nuclear suppliers may be required to pay deferred payments under this section.
specifically refers to this section; and
explicitly repeals, alters, amends, modifies, impairs, displaces, or supersedes the effect of this subsection.

children.heading: Payments to and by the United States
children.label: h Payments to and by the United States

children.heading: Notification
children.label: A Notification
children.text: In the case of a request for funds under Article VII of the Convention resulting from a covered incident that is not a Price-Anderson incident, the Secretary shall notify each nuclear supplier of the amount of the deferred payment required to be made by the nuclear supplier.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided under clause (ii), not later than 60 days after receipt of a notification under subparagraph (A), a nuclear supplier shall pay to the general fund of the Treasury the deferred payment of the nuclear supplier required under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Annual payments
children.label: ii Annual payments
children.text: A nuclear supplier may elect to prorate payment of the deferred payment required under subparagraph (A) in 5 equal annual payments (including interest on the unpaid balance at the prime rate prevailing at the time the first payment is due).

children.heading: Vouchers
children.label: C Vouchers
children.text: A nuclear supplier shall submit payment certification vouchers to the Secretary of the Treasury in accordance with .section 3325 of title 31

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Amounts paid into the Treasury under paragraph (1) shall be available to the Secretary of the Treasury, without further appropriation and without fiscal year limitation, for the purpose of making the contributions of public funds required to be made by the United States under the Convention.

children.heading: Action by Secretary of Treasury
children.label: B Action by Secretary of Treasury
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall pay the contribution required under the Convention to the court of competent jurisdiction under Article XIII of the Convention with respect to the applicable covered incident.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the payment due from the nuclear supplier;

children.label: B
children.text: any applicable interest on the payment; and

children.label: C
children.text: a penalty of not more than twice the amount of the deferred payment due from the nuclear supplier.

children.heading: Limitation on judicial review; cause of action
children.label: i Limitation on judicial review; cause of action

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In any civil action arising under the Convention over which Article XIII of the Convention grants jurisdiction to the courts of the United States, any appeal or review by writ of mandamus or otherwise with respect to a nuclear incident that is not a Price-Anderson incident shall be in accordance with chapter 83 of title 28, except that the appeal or review shall occur in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.

children.heading: Supreme Court jurisdiction
children.label: B Supreme Court jurisdiction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph affects the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court of the United States under chapter 81 of title 28.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), in any civil action arising under the Convention over which Article XIII of the Convention grants jurisdiction to the courts of the United States, in addition to any other cause of action that may exist, an individual or entity shall have a cause of action against the operator to recover for nuclear damage suffered by the individual or entity.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall apply only if the individual or entity seeks a remedy for nuclear damage (as defined in Article I of the Convention) that was caused by a nuclear incident (as defined in Article I of the Convention) that is not a Price-Anderson incident.

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: C Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph may be construed to limit, modify, extinguish, or otherwise affect any cause of action that would have existed in the absence of enactment of this paragraph.

children.heading: Right of recourse
children.label: j Right of recourse
children.text: This section does not provide to an operator of a covered installation any right of recourse under the Convention.

children.heading: Protection of sensitive United States information
children.label: k Protection of sensitive United States information

children.text: any data that, at any time, was Restricted Data (as defined in );section 2014 of this title
information relating to intelligence sources or methods protected by ; orsection 3024(h) of title 50
national security information classified under Executive Order 12958 ([former] note; relating to classified national security information) (or a successor Executive Order or regulation).50 U.S.C. 435

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary or the Commission, as appropriate, may prescribe regulations to carry out and this section.section 2210 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the implementation of and this section is consistent and equitable; andsection 2210 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the financial and operational burden on a Commission licensee in complying with is not greater as a result of the enactment of this section.section 2210 of this title

children.heading: Applicability of provision
children.label: 3 Applicability of provision
children.text: shall apply with respect to the promulgation of regulations under this subsection.Section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Effect of subsection
children.label: 4 Effect of subsection
children.text: The authority provided under this subsection is in addition to, and does not impair or otherwise affect, any other authority of the Secretary or the Commission to prescribe regulations.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: m Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect on .December 19, 2007
